item.title: Hospital Security Beyond Patching: Testing Workflow and Identity Risk
item.description: Hospital penetration testing isn’t just about patching. Learn how workflow, identity, and Active Directory risks create real breach paths in healthcare environments.

item.title: PCI Compliance in Higher Education: Why Your Campus Payment Systems Need Annual Penetration Testing
item.description: Campuses operate like small, decentralized cities, with numerous departments and subject areas generating millions in annual revenue from tuition, bookstore sales, dining hall purchases, and stadium concessions. While this diverse ecosystem enriches campus life, the fragmentation creates a massive and complex attack surface that is highly vulnerable to hackers. While higher education institutions generally maintain […]

item.title: AD-RECON Part II: Analyzing Attack Paths and Transitive Rights
item.description: Uncover hidden BloodHound attack paths without manual graph analysis. Learn how AD-RECON's advanced pathing and transitive queries reveal routes to Domain Admin.

item.title: Smarter Content Discovery Using Burp Suite
item.description: Context discovery is a critical part of an in-depth application testing methodology. This is an area where loads of risk can be uncovered that web application scanners and past security assessments might have missed. At BreakPoint Labs (BPL), we place a strong focus on application content discovery. This blog post will share our approach to […]

item.title: How to Triage BloodHound Data Faster: The AD-RECON Guide
item.description: Streamline your BloodHound analysis with AD-RECON. Learn how to automate Neo4j queries to quickly triage AD escalation paths and find quick wins. Watch now.

item.title: The Attacker’s Playbook, Part 4: The Maze Within
item.description: Automated scanners can't see the complex web of Active Directory trust. Learn how attackers exploit "GenericAll" permissions and the "Domain Users" group to escalate privileges in this guide to securing internal networks.

item.title: The Attacker’s Playbook, Part 3: The Keys to the Kingdom
item.description: Learn how default credentials and misplaced PHI expose healthcare networks. Part 3 of The Attacker's Playbook covers Active Directory security, gMSA implementation, and data governance strategies to lock down the "keys to the kingdom."

item.title: The Attacker’s Playbook, Part 2: The Unlocked Door
item.description: Your digital defense is only as strong as your physical security. From malicious USB devices in patient rooms to exposed pager apps, discover how attackers exploit the "forgotten front door" in healthcare.

item.title: The Attacker’s Playbook, Part 1: The Human Element is the Attacker’s Greatest Advantage
item.description: It wasn't a zero-day exploit—it was a simple text file. Discover how human error can compromise an entire healthcare network in Part 1 of "The Attacker's Playbook."

item.title: Closing Critical Cybersecurity Gaps in Healthcare: Lessons from an HHS-OIG Audit
item.description: In July 2025, HHS-OIG released a cybersecurity audit of a major hospital, with technical testing by BreakPoint Labs. We break down the findings—from weak authentication to web app flaws—and provide a 30/60/90-day action plan for healthcare leaders.

item.title: Hunting for Escalation Paths in AD: AdminCount
item.description: A guide to finding Active Directory privilege escalation paths using AdminCount analysis. We explore how to use BloodHound and Python to identify unprotected accounts (AdminCount: False) that expose the domain to takeover.

item.title: AI Red Teaming Part 3 – Attacking Claude Code using PyRIT
item.description: Learn how to automate image prompt injection attacks against Claude Code using PyRIT. We explore how malicious instructions hidden in git repository images can trigger unauthorized command execution in Anthropic's CLI tool.
